cards.back: A machine learning model inspired by the human brain, consisting of layers of interconnected nodes (neurons) that process information.
A mathematical notation describing the upper bound of an algorithm's time or space complexity as input size grows.
A data structure that maps keys to values using a hash function for O(1) average-case lookups.
Representational State Transfer - an architectural style for designing networked applications using HTTP methods.
A programming technique where a function calls itself to solve smaller instances of the same problem.
A type of ML where the model learns from labeled training data to make predictions on new data.
When a model learns the training data too well, including noise, resulting in poor generalization to new data.
A function that measures how well a model's predictions match the actual values, guiding the training process.
An optimization algorithm that iteratively adjusts model parameters to minimize the loss function.
A technique to evaluate model performance by splitting data into multiple train/test sets and averaging results.
A lightweight copy of the actual DOM that React uses to efficiently update only changed elements.
A CSS layout model that allows responsive elements within a container to be automatically arranged.
Cross-Origin Resource Sharing - a security mechanism that allows or restricts web requests across different domains.
A protocol providing full-duplex communication channels over a single TCP connection for real-time data.
Server-Side Rendering - rendering web pages on the server before sending HTML to the client for faster initial loads.
